task_completion.description: Create the VLCC vessel model directory and configure boat.txt with the required specifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output showing the file '/opt/bridgecommand/Models/VLCC_Training/boat.txt'. The file must contain the specified parameters: Length 330, Beam 60, Draft 21.5, Mass 340000, 1 Engine, Max Speed 15.5, Max Rudder 35, Rudder Speed 2.5, Astern Efficiency 0.3, Buffet Factor 0.2, Propeller Walk (Ahead -0.02, Astern 0.05), GPS (0, 40, 3), Depth Sounder (0, -150, -20), and Bridge View (0, 30, 160).

task_completion.description: Include suitable 3D model files from an existing large ship in the new vessel directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager or terminal 'ls' command showing the contents of '/opt/bridgecommand/Models/VLCC_Training/'. There should be 3D model files (e.g., .x, .obj, or similar geometry/texture files) copied from another vessel.

task_completion.description: Create the training scenario directory and file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the creation of the directory '/opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/n) VLCC Channel Approach/' and a scenario file (typically .ini or .txt) within it.

task_completion.description: Configure the scenario with the correct environment, ownship, and traffic vessels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of the scenario file in a text editor or terminal. It must show the environment set to a daytime Solent/Channel, the ownship set to 'VLCC_Training', and at least 2 other traffic vessels defined.

task_completion.description: Document the configured parameters in a report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/vlcc_config_report.txt' containing a summary of the VLCC parameters configured in the task.

integrity.description: Ensure files are created in the correct absolute system paths.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal commands or file manager paths to verify the agent operated in '/opt/bridgecommand/' for the models and scenarios, rather than mocking these directories in the user's home folder.

integrity.description: Ensure 3D model files are genuinely copied from an existing ship.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the copy commands (e.g., 'cp -r') or file sizes in the VLCC_Training directory. The files should be real model/texture files, not empty dummy files created with 'touch'.

integrity.description: Ensure the configuration report is actually saved to disk.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence that '/home/ga/Documents/vlcc_config_report.txt' was saved (e.g., no unsaved indicator in the GUI text editor, or a successful write/save command in nano/vim).

privileged_info_for_vlm: MT Pacific Titan is a fictional VLCC created for this simulation, but its specifications are based on verified real-world maritime data. Length: 330.0m (tolerance 5.0m), Beam: 60.0m (tolerance 2.0m), Draft: 21.5m (tolerance 1.0m), Mass/Displacement: 340,000 tonnes (tolerance 10,000t), Max Speed: 15.5 knots (tolerance 0.5), Number of Engines: 1, Max Rudder Angle: 35 degrees. Other parameters (RudderSpeed, AsternEfficiency, PropWalk, BuffetFactor) are specific to the Bridge Command simulator engine.
